scenario: Lobsters work session - the shared vote/score recalculation (tight)

steps.step: Locate the recalculation and how its target is resolved

map_quality.rationale: Pins the call site exactly ('app/models/vote.rb:181 — t.update_score_and_recalculate!') and the target resolution ('Vote#target at app/models/vote.rb:185-191 — returns Comment.find(comment_id) when comment_id is present, else Story.find(story_id)'), plus the enclosing method — agent needs no extra exploration.

specificity.rationale: All anchors are concrete file:line (vote.rb:181 call site, vote.rb:177 't = v.target', vote.rb:185-191 definition) rather than file-only or prose.

justification.rationale: States the dispatch is polymorphic ('two implementations dispatched by the target's class') and shows the comment_id-vs-story_id branch that decides which definition runs, making clear why the call site can't statically resolve it.

uncertainty.rationale: No hop is explicitly flagged as unopened; the answer reads as fully confident, with only a 'documented race' caveat that isn't about its own coverage.

relationship.rationale: Explicitly surfaces the polymorphic resolution: target is one of two record kinds (Comment.find(comment_id) else Story.find(story_id)), and that choice decides which update_score_and_recalculate! runs — the chain is stated, not just the endpoints.

steps.step: Document what the recalculation recomputes and persists

map_quality.rationale: Pins both divergent definitions exactly: Story#update_score_and_recalculate! at story.rb:693-718 writing score/flags/hotness via calculated_hotness (story.rb:550), and Comment#update_score_and_recalculate! at comment.rb:339-363 writing score/flags/confidence/confidence_order via calculated_confidence (comment.rb:232). No conflation.

specificity.rationale: Every method, column, and value-method is pinned with exact file:line (comment.rb:232, comment.rb:339-363, story.rb:550, story.rb:693-718) including the interpolation sites (story.rb:715, comment.rb:359-360).

justification.rationale: Explains the per-kind divergence by listing what each SQL writes (flagger-commented exemption for story, confidence_order packed key for comment), making clear why per-thread confidence and front-page hotness are different computations, though it doesn't frame it explicitly as 'thread-local vs front-page ranking'.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the documented race-condition caveat shared by both methods, but doesn't explicitly mark any hop as unverified or list things it couldn't confirm.

relationship.rationale: Keeps the two branches cleanly distinct under a 'polymorphic' header, attributing hotness/calculated_hotness exclusively to Story and confidence/confidence_order/calculated_confidence exclusively to Comment, with no cross-contamination.

steps.step: Map the change and the specs that cover it

map_quality.rationale: Names exactly what to edit (story.rb:693-718, comment.rb:339-363), the preserved call site (vote.rb:181), and lists the spec coverage with file:line ranges across vote_spec, story_spec, comment_spec, and bitpacking_spec. Also flags other callers (comment.rb:484/543, hidden_story.rb:15/21) the agent must not break.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every item has concrete file:line (e.g., 'spec/models/story_spec.rb:404-422 — update_score_and_recalculate group', 'spec/extras/bitpacking_spec.rb:145-160'). No vague 'add tests'.

justification.rationale: Each spec entry is annotated with what it guards (score/flag deltas, calculated_hotness values, confidence_order byte packing, flagger-commented exemption), and the regression_risk paragraph ties the edits to the specific specs that would catch breakage.

uncertainty.rationale: Does not explicitly flag what it is least sure about; the 'shared_caveat' on the race is observational, not a calibration of confidence. No 'unsure whether X is covered' note despite mixing line ranges with single-line citations.

relationship.rationale: Keeps each branch tied to its own definition, value method, columns, and spec: Story→calculated_hotness/hotness→story_spec; Comment→calculated_confidence/confidence_order→comment_spec + bitpacking_spec. No conflation between the two definitions.
